exercises.question: Choose the word that has the underlined part pronounced differently: stress, relaxed, event, helpline
exercises.explanation: 'event' has /ɪ/ in the first syllable, while A, B, D have /e/ sound.

exercises.question: Choose the word that has the underlined part pronounced differently: confident, cognitive, face, creative
exercises.explanation: 'face' has /s/ sound for underlined 'c', while A, B, D have /k/ sound for underlined 'c'.

exercises.question: Choose the word that its main stress is placed differently: habit, delighted, music, pressure
exercises.explanation: 'delighted' has stress on the second syllable; A, C, D are stressed on the first.

exercises.question: Choose the word that its main stress is placed differently: behave, wonderful, prepare, tradition
exercises.explanation: 'wonderful' has stress on the first syllable; A, C, D are stressed on the second.

exercises.question: _______ our school is small, it has strict rules.
exercises.explanation: 'Although' introduces a concessive clause (despite the fact that).

exercises.question: She is a talented _______. Her songs are widely listened to by young people.
exercises.explanation: 'musician' (noun for a person) follows the article 'a talented'.

exercises.question: The children _______ my home village used to go barefoot, even in winter. Now they all have shoes.
exercises.explanation: 'the children in my home village' - 'in' for location.

exercises.question: We all wish there _______ a smart board in our classroom now.
exercises.explanation: Wish + past simple/subjunctive for present wishes. 'were' is correct.

exercises.question: Every country has its own customs and _______.
exercises.explanation: 'customs and traditions' is a common collocation.

exercises.question: I used _______ to school but now I cycle to school.
exercises.explanation: 'used to + V-infinitive' for past habits.

exercises.question: She has failed her driving test three times. She feels _______.
exercises.explanation: Failing three times would make someone feel 'depressed' (sad/disappointed).

exercises.question: The Internet is a vast _______ of computers, all connected together.
exercises.explanation: The Internet is a 'network' of computers.

exercises.question: The children started to _______ each part of the story.
exercises.explanation: 'act out' means to perform/dramatize a story.

exercises.question: My teacher said I can talk to her about my project anytime. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Reported speech backshifting rule: when the reporting verb is past ('said'), modal verbs shift back: 'can' -> 'could', 'will' -> 'would', 'may' -> 'might'. So 'said I can' should be 'said I could'. Exception: you MAY keep original tense if the situation is still true, but for exam purposes, always backshift.

exercises.question: I prefer talking face to face to talking in the phone. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'on the phone' (not 'in the phone').

exercises.question: Minh is interested in basketball, but he isn't enough tall. (Find the underlined part that needs correction)
exercises.explanation: Should be 'tall enough' (adjective + enough, not enough + adjective).

exercises.question: "I have bought you a toy. Happy birthday to you!" "_______"
options: B. What a lovely toy! Thanks.
exercises.explanation: Expressing gratitude for a gift: compliment the gift and thank.

exercises.question: Mom: "Don't stay up late, or you'll be exhausted the next day." Mary: "_______"
exercises.explanation: 'No, I won't' (stay up late) = agreeing to follow the advice.

exercises.question: Come and join us at our local festival this weekend. I'm sure you won't regret it.
exercises.explanation: 'local' (nearby, regional) is the opposite of 'international' (worldwide).

exercises.question: My mother often tells me never to accept a lift from someone I've just met!
exercises.explanation: 'accept' (take/receive) is the opposite of 'refuse' (reject/decline).

exercises.question: The Internet is available in cities and towns, so we cannot easily get access to it in the countryside.
exercises.explanation: 'available' means existing and accessible = 'existing'.

exercises.question: She could be in an awful mood and refuse to answer my questions.
exercises.explanation: 'awful' means very bad, terrible.

exercises.passage: Located (23) _______ Giao Thuy district, the Countryside Museum (24) _______ a popular attraction for visitors to Nam Dinh Province. The museum was established by a retired teacher, Mrs. Khieu, while working as a teacher, she liked (25) _______ household utensils of residents in the Tonkin region. (26) _______ the 1990s, she has preserved objects that people, particular farmers, regularly used in the old days, so that younger generations can learn about these objects. Knowing Mrs. Khieu's good (27) _______, many people have brought her daily used subjects in rural areas, such as water buckets, different types of brooms, bronze and wooden trays and fishing baskets.

questions.question: Fill blank (23)
questions.explanation: 'Located in' a district - preposition of location.

questions.question: Fill blank (24)
questions.explanation: 'the museum is a popular attraction' - singular subject.

questions.question: Fill blank (25)
questions.explanation: 'collecting household utensils' - she liked gathering objects.

exercises.passage: New York is called "the Big Apple". Maybe it is not exactly like an apple, it's certainly very big. There are too many people, that's the problem. The streets are always full of cars and trucks; you can never find a place to park. Travelling around is never easy in the city. If you have enough money, you can take a taxi. New York cabs are yellow. They look all the same. But the drivers are very different. Some were born and raised in New York, but many are newcomers to the United States. A few drive slowly, but most go very fast. Cab driving is a difficult job. It can be dangerous, too. Thieves often try to steal the drivers' money. Drivers sometimes get hurt. If you don't want to take a taxi, you can go by bus or you can take the subway. The subway is quick and it's cheap, but some of its parts are old and dirty. Lights don't always work and there are often fires on the track. On some subway lines, there are new, clean and silver trains. But you can't see the color of the old trains easily. There is too much dirt and too much graffiti, inside and outside.

questions.question: You may find it hard to see _______.
options: A. new, clean and silver trains
C. the color of the old trains
questions.explanation: 'you can't see the color of the old trains easily' due to dirt and graffiti.

questions.question: What does "a cab" mean?
questions.explanation: The passage equates cabs with taxis: 'New York cabs are yellow'.

questions.question: What is the problem in New York?
options: A. It looked like an apple
B. It has too many apples.
questions.explanation: 'There are too many people, that's the problem.' = too crowded.

questions.question: Cab drivers in New York _______.
options: C. can be attacked by thieves
D. were all born in New York
questions.explanation: 'Thieves often try to steal the drivers' money. Drivers sometimes get hurt.'

questions.question: The word "subway" can be replaced by _______.

exercises.question: Ian didn't know how to water-ski, but he tried to do it. (Choose the sentence with the same meaning)
options: A. Although Ian tried hard, he didn't know how to water-ski.
B. Although Ian didn't know how to water-ski, he tried to do it.
C. Ian didn't water-ski because he didn't know how to.
D. Ian didn't try to water-ski because he didn't know how to do it.
exercises.explanation: 'Although' preserves both facts: not knowing + still trying.

exercises.question: The picnic was cancelled because of the heavy rain. (Choose the sentence with the same meaning)
options: A. If it hadn't rained so heavily, the picnic wouldn't be cancelled.
B. If it hadn't rained, the picnic would have been cancelled.
C. But for the heavy rain, the picnic wouldn't be cancelled.
D. But for the heavy rain, the picnic wouldn't have been cancelled.
exercises.explanation: Conditional type 3 for unreal past (Grammar Rule: If + past perfect, would have + past participle). 'But for' = 'If it hadn't been for'. Since the picnic WAS cancelled (past fact), the imaginary opposite uses type 3: 'But for the heavy rain, the picnic wouldn't have been cancelled.' Option A is wrong because 'wouldn't be cancelled' mixes type 2 result with type 3 condition.

exercises.question: "If I were you, I would take a break," Tom said to Daisy. (Choose the sentence with the same meaning)
options: A. Tom suggested not taking a break.
B. Tom wanted to take a break with Daisy.
C. Tom advised Daisy to take a break.
D. Tom wanted to take a break, and so did Daisy.
exercises.explanation: 'If I were you, I would...' is advice. Reported: 'advised + object + to-infinitive'.

exercises.question: I wish I didn't have to get up early tomorrow. (Choose the sentence with the same meaning)
options: A. It's my wish to get up early tomorrow.
B. I have to get up early tomorrow.
C. Getting up early tomorrow is not necessary.
D. I don't have to get up early tomorrow.
exercises.explanation: 'I wish I didn't have to' implies the reality IS that I have to get up early.

exercises.question: engine/car/need/clean/once/month/. (Choose the best sentence from cues)
options: A. The engine of the car needs cleaning once a month.
B. The engine for the car need to clean once a month.
C. The engine with the car needs to be cleaned once a month.
D. The engine of the car needs to clean once a month.
exercises.explanation: 'need + V-ing' = 'need to be + V-ed' (passive meaning). A is correct and natural.

exercises.question: I/not/know/what/do/make/mother/happy/. (Choose the best sentence from cues)
options: A. I don't know what doing to make my mother happy.
B. I don't know what to doing make my mother happy.
C. I not know what do to make mother happy
D. I don't know what to do to make my mother happy.
exercises.explanation: 'what to do to make' - question word + to-infinitive + purpose infinitive.

exercises.question: Nobody/can/deny/that/she/talent/arts/. (Choose the best sentence from cues)
options: A. Nobody can deny that she talented of arts.
B. Nobody can deny that she's talent at arts.
C. Nobody can deny that she's talented with arts.
D. Nobody can deny that she's talented at arts.
exercises.explanation: 'talented at' is the correct preposition collocation.

exercises.question: many/place/the world/people/used/dye/cloth/nature/material/. (Choose the best sentence from cues)
options: A. In many places in the world, people used to dye cloth with nature materials.
B. In many places in the world, people used to dyeing cloth by natural materials.
C. In many places in the world, people used to dye cloth with natural materials.
D. In many places in the world, people used dyeing cloth by natural materials.
exercises.explanation: 'used to + V-infinitive' and 'with natural materials' (adjective form, correct preposition).

exercises.original: Ian didn't know how to water-ski, but he tried to do it.
exercises.answer: Although Ian didn't know how to water-ski, he tried to do it.
acceptedAnswers: Although Ian didn't know how to water-ski, he tried to do it.
Although Ian didn't know how to water-ski, he tried to do it
Although Ian didn't know how to water ski, he tried to do it.
exercises.explanation: Dùng 'Although' để nối hai vế tương phản. 'didn't know... but he tried' → 'Although Ian didn't know how to water-ski, he tried to do it'.

exercises.original: The picnic was cancelled because of the heavy rain.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with: If it hadn't
exercises.answer: If it hadn't rained so heavily, the picnic wouldn't have been cancelled.
acceptedAnswers: If it hadn't rained so heavily, the picnic wouldn't have been cancelled.
If it hadn't rained so heavily, the picnic wouldn't have been cancelled
If it hadn't rained heavily, the picnic wouldn't have been cancelled.
exercises.explanation: Chuyển 'because of' sang câu điều kiện loại 3 (điều trái quá khứ). 'because of the heavy rain' → 'If it hadn't rained so heavily... wouldn't have been cancelled'.

exercises.original: "If I were you, I would take a break," Tom said to Daisy.
exercises.answer: Tom advised Daisy to take a break.
acceptedAnswers: Tom advised Daisy to take a break.
Tom advised Daisy to take a break
exercises.explanation: Chuyển lời khuyên trực tiếp sang gián tiếp. '"If I were you, I would take a break"' → 'Tom advised Daisy to take a break'.

exercises.original: I wish I didn't have to get up early tomorrow.
exercises.instruction: Rewrite beginning with: I have to
exercises.answer: I have to get up early tomorrow.
acceptedAnswers: I have to get up early tomorrow.
I have to get up early tomorrow
exercises.explanation: 'Wish + didn't have to' diễn tả điều ước trái với thực tại. 'wish I didn't have to' → thực tế là 'I have to get up early tomorrow'.

exercises.prompt: engine / car / need / clean / once / month
exercises.answer: The engine of the car needs cleaning once a month.
acceptedAnswers: The engine of the car needs cleaning once a month.
The engine of the car needs cleaning once a month
The engine of the car needs to be cleaned once a month.
exercises.explanation: Need + V-ing (passive meaning) or need + to be + past participle.

exercises.prompt: I / not / know / what / do / make / mother / happy
exercises.answer: I don't know what to do to make my mother happy.
acceptedAnswers: I don't know what to do to make my mother happy.
I don't know what to do to make my mother happy
I do not know what to do to make my mother happy.
exercises.explanation: Question word + to-infinitive as object; to-infinitive for purpose.

exercises.prompt: Nobody / can / deny / that / she / talent / arts
exercises.answer: Nobody can deny that she is talented at arts.
acceptedAnswers: Nobody can deny that she is talented at arts.
Nobody can deny that she's talented at arts.
Nobody can deny that she is talented at arts
exercises.explanation: Be talented at + noun for ability in a field.

exercises.prompt: many / place / the world / people / used / dye / cloth / natural / material
exercises.answer: In many places in the world, people used to dye cloth with natural materials.
acceptedAnswers: In many places in the world, people used to dye cloth with natural materials.
In many places in the world, people used to dye cloth with natural materials
In many places of the world, people used to dye cloth with natural materials.
exercises.explanation: Used to + bare infinitive for past habits; 'with' for materials used.
